title: 10 x Flat Joining Plates (100 x 40 x 2) Connecting Perforated Metal Brace Bracket Timber Wood Fixing Galvanised Steel Repair Connector
features: 📐 PACKAGE CONTENTS: 10 x Flat joining brackets; SIZE: 100 x 40 mm, THICKNESS: 2 mm; HOLES: 10 x Ø 5mm. Screws are not included.

✅QUALITY MATERIAL: The galvanized perforated plates are made of high-quality 2 .0 mm thick galvanised steel sheet and enable a robust and long-lasting connection. Its structure provides strength and corrosion resistance, making it an excellent choice for both indoor and outdoor applications.

🦾 ROBUST & DURABLE: The staggered hole pattern enhances the overall strength and stability of the joint. By offsetting the holes in each row, the plates offer improved load-bearing capacity and distribute the forces more evenly across the joint. This design feature helps to prevent stress concentrations and minimizes the risk of splitting or failure.
🛠️ EASY INSTALLATION: The pre-drilled holes on the plate facilitate easy and secure fastening, using screws or bolts.

🏚️ VERSATILE APPLICATION: Our galvanized perforated plates are suitable for a variety of wood construction applications, such as building furniture, shelves, indoor and outdoor wood structures, as well as DIY projects.
description: Product Description Flat Joining Perforated Plates 100 x 40 x 2 mm Flat Joining Perforated Plates for wood with a staggered hole pattern are hardware components specifically designed to provide a secure and precise connection between wooden panels or pieces. These plates feature a flat metal sheet with evenly spaced perforations or holes arranged in a staggered formation. Specs: Package included: 10 x Flat Joining Plates Lenght: 100 mm; Width: 40 mm; Thickness: 2 mm; Holes diameter: 10 x ø5 mm; Material: Galvanised Steel; Weight of package: 0.570 kg. APPLICATION These plates are commonly used in various woodworking applications, ensuring stability, longevity and professional results. A quick and very easy system for joining Railway Sleepers. QUALITY The staggered hole pattern enhances the overall strength and stability of the joint. By offsetting the holes in each row, the plates offer improved load-bearing capacity and distribute the forces more evenly across the joint. This design feature helps to prevent stress concentrations and minimizes the risk of splitting or failure. DIMENSIONS The size and shape of the holes are typically standardized to accommodate commonly used screw sizes and fasteners in woodworking. The spacing between the holes is carefully determined to ensure optimal support and even distribution of forces.
